name: Extend From Every Number
summary: From each number, walk upward while the next value is still present in the array.
visualizationIntent: init: the raw array is shown, longest = 0; loop(num): the anchor value num is marked special; update: current starts at num (current cell) and length = 1; loop(while): the probe value current+1 is marked compared â€” if some cell holds it the run extends; move: current climbs, the highlighted head slides to the next value; update(longest): the best run length is refreshed via the readout; return: the array rests and LONGEST RUN holds the answer.

lineExplanations.3: If the array is empty there is no sequence to measure.
lineExplanations.5: Track the longest run length found so far.
lineExplanations.6: Try every value in the array as the start of a run.
lineExplanations.7: Begin walking upward from this value.
lineExplanations.8: The run currently has length 1 â€” just this value.
lineExplanations.9: While the next consecutive value can be found in the array...
lineExplanations.10: Step up to that next value.
lineExplanations.11: The run has grown by one.
lineExplanations.12: Keep the best run length seen so far.
lineExplanations.13: Return the length of the longest consecutive run.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the wrapper class the platform runs.
syntaxExplanations.2: def longestConsecutive(self, nums): the method receiving the numbers.
syntaxExplanations.3: if not nums: is True when the list is empty.
syntaxExplanations.6: for num in nums iterates over each value.
syntaxExplanations.7: current = num copies the starting value into a walker.
syntaxExplanations.9: current + 1 in nums scans the list to test membership.
syntaxExplanations.10: current += 1 advances the walker to the next value.
syntaxExplanations.11: length += 1 counts the newly reached value.
syntaxExplanations.12: max(longest, length) keeps whichever is larger.
syntaxExplanations.13: return longest hands back the answer.
